title: elevator_rescue
description: A passenger elevator stalls between floors with multiple occupants trapped inside.

timeline.title: Elevator Stalls
timeline.description: Elevator stops between floors 4 and 5. Occupants activate emergency alarm. Establish contact.

timeline.title: Mechanical Assessment
timeline.description: Building engineer confirms power failure. Elevator is resting on safety brakes. Evacuation possible from floor 4.

timeline.title: Manual Override Initiated
timeline.description: Fire crew uses elevator key to manually release the car to floor 4 landing.

timeline.title: Door Opened
timeline.description: Outer and inner doors opened. Occupants assisted out. One occupant has a panic attack.

timeline.title: Scene Cleared
timeline.description: All occupants out. Elevator isolated. Medical assessment completed. Building engineer notified.

node-1.title: Elevator Stalled Between Floors
node-1.situation: An elevator in a 10-storey office building has stalled between floors 4 and 5 during a power interruption. Six occupants are inside, including one person who is claustrophobic and becoming increasingly distressed. The building engineer is not on site.
node-1.question: What is your first action?

options.label: A
options.text: Establish two-way communication with the occupants via the emergency intercom, confirm everyone is uninjured, and advise them to remain calm and not attempt to exit on their own.

options.label: B
options.text: Immediately attempt to pry the outer doors open from the corridor.

options.label: C
options.text: Tell the occupants to push open the roof hatch and climb out.

node-2.title: Car Position and Power Status
node-2.situation: The occupants confirm they are uninjured. Power has failed to the elevator system. The car is resting on its safety brakes approximately 1.2 metres below the floor 4 landing. The building engineer is 30 minutes away.
node-2.question: How do you proceed?

options.label: A
options.text: Request the building engineer's remote guidance and use the firefighter emergency key to manually release the car to the nearest floor.

options.label: B
options.text: Wait 30 minutes for the building engineer to arrive before taking any action.

options.label: C
options.text: Cut power to the entire building to reset the elevator system.

node-3.title: Manual Car Movement
node-3.situation: Using the firefighter emergency key in the machine room, a crew member begins manually moving the car toward the floor 4 landing. The car is moving in 15 cm increments. The claustrophobic occupant is hyperventilating.
node-3.question: How do you manage the occupant's medical situation while the rescue continues?

options.label: A
options.text: Maintain calm verbal communication with the occupant through the intercom, guide them through slow breathing, and continue moving the car steadily to the landing.

options.label: B
options.text: Pause the car movement and wait for EMS before continuing — the occupant may have a medical emergency.

options.label: C
options.text: Speed up the manual winding to get the occupants out faster despite safety margins.

node-4.title: Doors Opened and Occupants Assisted
node-4.situation: The car has reached the floor 4 landing. The outer and inner doors have been opened. There is a 15 cm sill gap between the car floor and the building floor. The claustrophobic occupant refuses to step across the gap.
node-4.question: How do you safely assist all occupants?

options.label: A
options.text: Position a firefighter inside the corridor to physically assist each occupant across the gap; use a step bridge if available, and offer calm reassurance to the reluctant occupant.

options.label: B
options.text: Tell the reluctant occupant to wait alone in the elevator while the others are evacuated.

node-5.title: Post-Rescue Medical Assessment
node-5.situation: All six occupants are out. The claustrophobic occupant is still hyperventilating and showing signs of a panic attack. Others appear physically uninjured but shaken.
node-5.question: What is the appropriate post-rescue action?

options.label: A
options.text: Have EMS assess all occupants; isolate the elevator and place an out-of-service notice; notify the building engineer and elevator maintenance contractor.

options.label: B
options.text: Clear the scene once the occupants walk away — no injuries means no further action required.

node-6.title: Scenario Complete
node-6.situation: All occupants have been safely rescued and assessed. The elevator has been isolated. The building engineer and maintenance contractor have been notified. Proper documentation of the incident has been completed.
